The '''MC140 ''Scythe''-class main battle cruiser''' was the primary [[Warship/Legends|warship]] of the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]] approximately one hundred years after the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Designed and produced by the famed [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]], the Scythe was a radical departure from normal [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] starship design. In addition to the unique front blade, the ''Scythe''-class was a mass-produced, standardized design, unlike prior generations of the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]].

These battle [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] saw extensive use during the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. Following the surrender of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] government at the end of the Sith–Imperial War, many Scythe battle cruisers saw continued service in the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] during their [[Second Imperial Civil War|operations]] against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Scythefront.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Scythe''-class compared to the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]].]]
The ''Scythe''-class battle [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser's]] name was in part due to the massive vertical blade centered on the bow of the [[Warship/Legends|warship]]. The ''Scythe''-class was heavily armed with advanced weaponry, including three heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, six standard turbolaser batteries, two heavy [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] batteries,<ref name="LECG" /> and forty [[Proton torpedo launcher/Legends|proton torpedo tubes]]<ref name="EGW" /> grouped in four [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] batteries with a total 160-warhead capacity. The majority of the Scythe's weapon batteries were mounted on the forward blade. This configuration was referred to as a "cross of fire" by [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] officials and [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]], and allowed the Scythe-class battle cruiser to focus its fire onto a singular point. While the "cross of fire" weapons arrangement proved deadly to almost any warship that fell into its crosshairs, this provided some drawbacks. The heavy concentration of weapons batteries on the forward [[Hull/Legends|hull]] meant that there was limited weaponry on the ship's broadsides and stern. Furthermore, massive power conduits from the ship's main [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] and numerous auxiliary reactors took up additional space.<ref name="LECG" />

The Scythe's main hull followed standard Mon Calamari design patterns.<ref name="LECG" /> The forward blade gave the warship a similar appearance to the then-ancient [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]].<ref name="SW:KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref> In addition to containing much of the ship's weapons, the blade was also the location of several [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bays. These hangars could carry 36 [[CF9 Crossfire starfighter]]s and four [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]].<ref name="LECG" /> A twenty-one meter-long [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]] could easily fly through the aperture of one of its hangars.<ref name="Indomitable, Part 1" />

==Role==
As the main capital ship of the Galactic Alliance, ''Scythe''-class was capable of engaging the [[Fel Empire]]'s [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] in direct combat, despite the fact that the [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battle cruiser]] was only a third of the size of the larger [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="SWL8:A">[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]]</ref> The ''Scythe''-class utilized its powerful engines to maneuver into a position where it could bring the "cross of fire" weapons batteries to bear on an enemy vessel. This massive barrage of turbolaser, ion cannon, and torpedo fire was capable of destroying larger vessels like the ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer. The main drawback of the cross of fire was that the Scythe's broadsides and stern were lightly armed, which forced a Scythe's crew to constantly keep the bow facing an enemy warship - though with its powerful engines, this was rarely a problem.<ref name="LECG" />

==History==
[[File:Breakout.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Scythe''-class battle cruiser with ''Tri-Scythe''-class frigate to the left, at the [[Battle of Caamas]] in [[130 ABY]].]]
The ''Scythe''-class battle cruiser was the culmination of a new design and shipbuilding philosophy undertaken by the Galactic Alliance during the years following the devastation of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]].<ref name="LECG" /> For a time, large multi-kilometer [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaughts]] like the [[Viscount-class Star Defender|Star Defender]] saw increased use in Galactic Alliance fleets.<ref name="SBP1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/SBPreview1|text=''Starship Battles'' Preview 1}}</ref> However, economic realities changed the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force|Defense Force]] shipbuilding mindset towards smaller, yet heavily armed and armored vessels.<ref name="LECG" /> A result of the changing military doctrine, the ''Scythe''-class first debuted in [[92 ABY]].<ref name="EGW" />

Like the majority of Galactic Alliance warships from prior generations, the ''Scythe''-class was designed and constructed at the [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]]. The Scythe was part of a larger shipbuilding program that included various other classes of combat [[Starship/Legends|starships]], including the [[ShaShore-class frigate|''ShaShore''-class frigate]] and the [[Sabertooth-class assault/rescue vessel|''Sabertooth''-class assault/rescue vessel]]. The success of the Scythe and the cross of fire design prompted Mon Calamarian engineers to also develop the [[Tri-Scythe-class frigate|''Tri-Scythe''-class frigate]], a smaller sister-design with a three blade-shaped superstructure.<ref name="LECG" />

[[File:PellaeonvsScythe-LECG.png|thumb|right|220px|A MC140 ''Scythe''-class cruiser exchanges fire with a ''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer.]]
As the Scythe battle cruiser was the Alliance's main warship, it saw extensive action during the [[Sith–Imperial War]], starting in [[127 ABY]]. Many of the battle cruisers were part of the combined Alliance fleet that fought at the [[Battle of Caamas]]. Caamas proved to be a major Imperial victory and the death knell for the Galactic Alliance government, as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]] managed to force [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Piers Petan]]—the overall Alliance fleet commander at Caamas—to surrender.<ref name="SWL8:A" /> However, the [[Core Fleet|Galactic Alliance Core Fleet]], under Admiral [[Gar Stazi]], refused to surrender. The majority of the Core Fleet, including many Scythe battle cruisers, escaped while engaged in a fighting withdrawal from Caamas.<ref name="Indomitable, Part 2">[[Legacy (2006) 21|''Legacy'' (2006) 21]]</ref>

After the surrender of the Galactic Alliance at the end of the Sith-Imperial War, the cruisers saw continued use by the remnants of the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force|Defense Force]], via the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]].<ref name="RSDHB" /><ref name="SWL0½">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]]</ref> Scythe battle cruisers were part of the Alliance task force that engaged the Empire's [[Outer Rim Third Fleet]] during the [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Second Imperial Civil War)|Battle of Dac]]. One such vessel, Stazi's flagship [[Indomitable (Scythe-class)|''Indomitable'']], was destroyed when the crippled warship was sacrificed in an effort to severely damage the Mon Calamari shipyards, and prevent them from being used by the Sith for new ship construction.<ref name="Indomitable, Part 2" />

Several Scythes escorted the captured [[Imperious-class Star Destroyer|''Imperious''-class Star Destroyer]] prototype following the battle. Many more battle cruisers were part of a large Alliance fleet under Admiral Stazi that participated in a joint operation with the [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s forces against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].<ref name="Renegade">[[Legacy (2006) 36|''Legacy'' (2006) 36]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Scythe_class_profile.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Portside view of the ''Scythe''-class.]]
The ''Scythe''-class battle cruiser was designed by [[Sean Cooke]], who was the designer for all of the Galactic Alliance and Imperial warships in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' comic series.<ref name="RSTFN">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24622293|board=Literature|thread=Galactic Alliance Remnant discussion thread (Possible Legacy Spoilers)|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2006-08-09|time=12:30 pm|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801191729/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/galactic-alliance-remnant-discussion-thread-possible-legacy-spoilers.24604377/page-2}}</ref>

The name for the warship was announced by [[Randy Stradley]] on the [[Dark Horse Comics]] message boards. Stradley also revealed that the wing-like structures housed starfighter launch bays and torpedo launchers.<ref name="RSDHB" /> He was also the first to hint that the Scythe battle cruiser was of Mon Calamari design.<ref name="RSTFN" /> This was finally confirmed in the ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''.
